channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
item.description: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How to keep an ancient text interesting to students? Make it a summer blockbuster
item.description: English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.
item.content:encoded:
English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.

(Image credit: Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal Pic/Melinda Sue Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Questions remain after fatal police shooting in Madison, Wisconsin
item.description: Police shot and killed a man who they say was stealing bicycles and checking car doors. The four officers involved have been placed on administrative leave.
item.content:encoded: Police shot and killed a man who they say was stealing bicycles and checking car doors. The four officers involved have been placed on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rizzo

item.title: Over 4 million people are no longer on SNAP since last July
item.description: SNAP participation has been declining consistently and rapidly since last July, when President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill into law.
item.content:encoded: SNAP participation has been declining consistently and rapidly since last July, when President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill into law.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Along Lake Superior, erosion threatens the dead and the living's memories
item.description: Low lying cemeteries near shorelines face threats of erosion as water levels rise. That is especially true for two cemeteries near Lake Superior, the largest of the country's Great Lakes.
item.content:encoded: Low lying cemeteries near shorelines face threats of erosion as water levels rise. That is especially true for two cemeteries near Lake Superior, the largest of the country's Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Sam Fuqua

item.title: Trump allies are poised to weaken protections for historic sites — not just in D.C.
item.description: A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.
item.content:encoded:
A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.

(Image credit: Jen Golbeck)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Republicans urge Justice Department to investigate former prosecutor Jack Smith
item.description: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.
item.content:encoded:
The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: DOT cranks up its campaign to strip bike lane references from federal websites
item.description: Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.
item.content:encoded:
Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Amid a big hiring push, police experts question ICE officer vetting
item.description: The recent fatal shootings by ICE officers in Texas and Maine come at a time when the federal agency has hired a huge number of officers quickly. Some law enforcement experts worry that this kind of a hiring spree can lead police agencies to cut corners in vetting new hires.
item.content:encoded:
The recent fatal shootings by ICE officers in Texas and Maine come at a time when the federal agency has hired a huge number of officers quickly. Some law enforcement experts worry that this kind of a hiring spree can lead police agencies to cut corners in vetting new hires.

(Image credit: Gregory Rec)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: The White House pushes healthy eating. It cut a landmark nutrition education program
item.description: Nutrition advocates and public health experts are mystified by the decision to eliminate SNAP-Ed, given the Trump administration's embrace of the Make America Healthy Again agenda.
item.content:encoded:
Nutrition advocates and public health experts are mystified by the decision to eliminate SNAP-Ed, given the Trump administration's embrace of the Make America Healthy Again agenda.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez
